home.name: Robert Bitlev
home.role: AI360 student, data analyst, and web tool developer
home.activityLine: I build web tools and interfaces and work with data.
home.studentLine: B.Sc. student in “AI360: Mathematics of Machine Learning” at St. Petersburg State University.

paragraphs: My name is Robert Bitlev. I study in the AI360 program at St. Petersburg State University. I enjoy using mathematics and algorithms to build useful, well-made digital tools.
I am especially interested in frontend development, Python-based data analysis, and interface design, where logic, usability, and careful implementation matter.
My recent work includes educational product analytics, forecasting, CRM-focused data analysis, and browser applications.
Thanks to competitive programming, I am used to solving difficult technical problems calmly and under time pressure.
I am currently looking for internships and project work where I can apply technical skills and analytical thinking and gain more experience working in a team.

home.educationIntro: The focus here is on computer science and programming.
home.achievementsIntro: This section highlights results in informatics and programming, along with the strongest interdisciplinary olympiad results.
home.projectsIntro: These projects are presented through the lens of software development, data work, and a product mindset.
home.skillsIntro: A concise overview of the stack and working style behind this track.

sidebar.title: Achievements
sidebar.heading: Olympiads in physics and informatics
bullets: Winner and prize-winner of the final stage of the All-Russian Olympiad for School Students in Physics.
Gold medalist at the IJSO in 2023 and 2024.
Winner and prize-winner of the regional stage of the All-Russian Olympiad in Informatics.

sidebar.title: Interests
sidebar.heading: Mathematics, ML, and development
sidebar.text: Interested in machine learning, data analysis, algorithmic problem solving, and applied development.

document.role: AI360 Student | Mathematics of Machine Learning
document.summary: Student in the “AI360: Mathematics of Machine Learning” program at the Faculty of Mathematics and Computer Science at St. Petersburg State University. Interested in machine learning, data analysis, and applied development; in academic and project work I have worked with large datasets, statistical analysis, forecasting, and practical software tools.

projects.title: Resume Builder
projects.subtitle: Static web app with PDF export
bullets: Built a résumé editor from a Figma mockup using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript: two layouts, in-place content editing, and responsive behavior in a single-page app.
Added localStorage persistence and browser-side PDF export so the current version can be edited, saved, reset, and exported without a backend.

projects.title: Support Service Workload Assessment
projects.subtitle: Forecasting technical support requests
bullets: Processed more than 600k support requests together with product navigation data, built user-profile and repeat-contact metrics, and studied how support is related to DAU growth.
Built a 12-week SARIMAX forecast of weekly workload and a logistic model of request probability for workload planning and resource allocation.

projects.title: Credit Profiling
projects.subtitle: DANO Hackathon, ITMO University
bullets: Preprocessed and aggregated client data, compared distributions across four credit products, and visualized relationships with histograms, box plots, scatter plots, and correlation heatmaps.
Identified factors behind product choice and turned the findings into audience-segmentation ideas and recommendations for three CRM scenarios: upsell, cross-sell, and down-sell.

projects.title: Brain Train
projects.subtitle: Interactive memory trainer on Arduino Nano
bullets: Designed an Arduino Nano device with random LED sequences, OLED feedback, a hint system, and button input for memory training.
Combined four Arduino C++ modules — game logic, button handling, display output, and LED patterns — and tuned the timing for smoother interaction.

courses: School of Practical Programming and Data Analysis

career.title: Career Path
career.name: Robert Bitlev
career.role: AI360 student, data analyst, and web tool developer

careerPath.intro: This IT path is built around competitive programming, data projects, and the AI360 program in mathematics and machine learning.

pillars.title: Academic foundation
pillars.text: AI360 at St. Petersburg State University brings together mathematics, computer science, and machine learning and provides the academic basis for this path.

pillars.title: Practical development
pillars.text: JavaScript, Python, SQL, C++, and small product-oriented tools make up the applied side of the portfolio.

pillars.title: Olympiad experience
pillars.text: Olympiads in informatics, physics, and mathematics taught me to solve difficult problems under time pressure.

timeline.title: Lyceum No. 533, St. Petersburg
timeline.text: Completed secondary education with a GPA of 5.00/5.00.

timeline.title: T-Generation, “Olympiad Mathematics” and “Algorithms and Data Structures”
timeline.text: Completed advanced training in olympiad mathematics and programming.

timeline.title: Olympiad results in mathematics and informatics
timeline.text: Won the regional stage of the All-Russian Olympiad in Informatics in 2024 and 2025; in 2025 also won Technocup and received a second-degree diploma in the Phystech Olympiad.

timeline.title: DANO Hackathon and credit profiling, ITMO University
timeline.text: Analyzed product and client data and turned statistical findings into CRM recommendations.

timeline.title: Practical programming and data analysis at HSE University
timeline.text: Worked with data from educational products, including the assessment and forecasting of support service workload.

timeline.title: JavaScript intensive and Resume Builder
timeline.text: Built a browser-based résumé editor with responsive layouts, localStorage persistence, and PDF export.

timeline.title: AI360 at St. Petersburg State University
timeline.text: Started a bachelor’s degree in “AI360: Mathematics of Machine Learning” at the Faculty of Mathematics and Computer Science.

highlights.title: Analytics and forecasting
highlights.text: I use Python for data analysis, automation, and building practical models.

highlights.title: Interface development
highlights.text: I build clean, easy-to-use web interfaces with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

highlights.title: Collaborative project work
highlights.text: I can work well in a team: discuss solutions, keep task constraints in mind, and bring work to a usable result.

highlights.title: Foreign languages
highlights.text: I use English at C1 level and German at B2-C1.

focus.title: Machine learning and data analysis
focus.text: I am deepening my knowledge of Python, SQL, statistics, and machine learning.

focus.title: Useful web tools
focus.text: I am learning JavaScript and web technologies to build tools that make websites more functional and easier to use.

focus.title: Technical communication
focus.text: I am working on explaining technical ideas clearly, discussing solutions, and collaborating effectively toward a shared result.
